text.title: VideoGenerationService and Production Rules
text.summary: Async VideoGenerationService with multi-provider routing, retry, download, and known production rules.

section.title: VideoGenerationService
section.reference: VideoGenerationService: async multi-provider with retry and local download
section.p: VideoGenerationService abstracts provider selection, retry logic, and post-generation download into a single async generate() call. The _upload_image method is a stub — must be implemented per storage backend before image-to-video works.

rule.statement: Download videos immediately after SUCCEEDED status. Pre-signed URLs from Runway and Luma expire in 30-60 minutes.
rule.rationale: An archival agent that sweeps output_dir hours later will receive 404 on all cached provider URLs. Download on success, then serve from local storage.

rule.statement: Use streaming HTTP download for video files to avoid loading entire files into memory.
rule.rationale: httpx async download that reads response.content buffers the full video before writing. A 100MB file breaks on memory-constrained hosts.

rule.statement: Log submitted prompt and task ID together at generation time. Store provider, model version, and seed for reproducibility.
rule.rationale: Re-querying a failed task requires the task ID. Debugging quality issues requires knowing exactly which model version and seed produced the output.

rule.statement: Validate output file exists and size is greater than 0 before returning success from the service.
rule.rationale: APIs can return SUCCEEDED status with empty output. Propagating an empty file path causes silent failures in downstream processing.

antipattern.description: Passing a Runway task ID to a Luma polling endpoint or vice versa.
antipattern.reason: Task IDs and generation IDs are provider-specific and non-portable. Keep provider context alongside each ID.
